agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New Burnaby Sign Bylaw and enforcement amendments
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed new Sign Bylaw that would replace the current rules for all signs on commercial and residential properties. The proposal also includes changes to the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, which is the system that handles fines and penalties for breaking city rules. If approved, this would affect how businesses display signs, including size, placement, and lighting. The new bylaw has not been passed yet — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed sign rules — they could affect your business signage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
PROPOSED NEW BURNABY SIGN BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for the proposed new Sign Bylaw and related amendments to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Strata title conversion for 4558 Portland Street duplex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to allow the owner of an existing two-family dwelling at 4558 Portland Street to convert it into strata lots. This means the building would be split into separate, individually owned units (like condos) instead of being owned as one single property. The decision is not final — Council will vote on whether to approve this conversion at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — this is a site-specific application that does not affect other properties.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
STRATA TITLE CONVERSION APPLICATION #25-02 - 4558 PORTLAND STREET
Purpose: To obtain Council authority for strata titling of an existing occupied two-family dwelling.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Time-limited changes to Burnaby rental housing rules for developers
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will vote on temporary amendments to the Rental Use Zoning Policy (Stream 2) for eligible RM District developments. For pre-construction projects, the inclusionary rental requirement would be calculated from the strata component only, and the required below-market rate would change from 20% below CMHC median rents to Burnaby-wide CMHC median market rents. For under-construction projects, the same rental-rate change applies. Eligibility deadlines: pre-construction projects must obtain a building permit by June 30, 2027 and commence construction by December 31, 2027; under-construction projects must commence or complete construction by December 31, 2027.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the June 9, 2026 council meeting and consider submitting comments if you are a developer with an eligible RM District project.

key_dates.label: Council vote on rental rule amendments — Burnaby
Building permit deadline for pre-construction projects — Burnaby RUZP

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
PROPOSED TIME LIMITED AMENDMENTS TO THE INCLUSIONARY RENTAL REQUIREMENTS OF THE RENTAL USE ZONING POLICY FOR ELIGIBLE DEVELOPMENTS
Purpose:
To seek Council approval of time-limited amendments to the inclusionary rental requirements (Stream 2) of the Rental Use Zoning Policy for eligible pre-construction and under-construction developments to encourage new housing supply.

attachments.title: Proposed Time Limited Amendments to the Inclusionary Rental Requirements of the

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on buying two aerial ladder fire trucks
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award to purchase two new aerial ladder fire trucks. These are the large fire trucks with extendable ladders used to fight fires in tall buildings. If approved, the contract would authorize the city to buy the trucks for the fire department. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - TWO AERIAL LADDER FIRE TRUCKS
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the purchase of two (2) aerial ladder fire trucks.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Kensington Park playground contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and installation of new playground structures at Kensington Park. If approved, the contract would go to a chosen company to build the playground. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet and will be decided at the upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - KENSINGTON PARK SUPPLY INSTALL OF PLAYGROUND STRUCTURES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Kensington Park – Supply and Install of Playground Structure.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on security system contract renewal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract renewal for the city's Security Incident Detection System. This system is used to monitor and detect security incidents in city facilities. If approved, the renewal would extend the existing contract for this technology. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CR - SECURITY INCIDENT DETECTION SYSTEM - RENEWAL
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Security Incident Detection System.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on renewing contract for holiday lights supply and maintenance
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed contract renewal for the supply, installation, maintenance, removal, and storage of LED holiday lights. If approved, this would extend the current arrangement for decorating public spaces during the holiday season. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CR - HOLIDAY LIGHTS (LED)
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Supply, Install, Maintenance, Removal and Storage of Holiday Lights.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Playground Development Grants Spring 2026 intake
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Spring 2026 intake for the Playground Development Grant Program. If approved, this program would provide funding to help build or upgrade playgrounds in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the recommendation at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - PLAYGROUND DEVELOPMENT GRANTS 2026
Purpose:
To seek Council approval for the Playground Development Grant Program, Spring 2026 intake recommendation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sport Hosting Grant Spring 2026 intake approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Spring 2026 intake of the Sport Hosting Grant Program. If approved, this program would provide funding to local sports organizations to help them bring sporting events to Burnaby. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, local sports groups can apply for funding to host events in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
ECC - SPORT HOSTING GRANT SPRING 2026 INTAKE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Sport Hosting Grant Program, Spring 2026 intake recommendation.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on lease extension for Pacific Assistance Dogs Society
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to amend the lease agreement with the Pacific Assistance Dogs Society (PADS) for their location at 9048 Stormont Avenue. The proposed change would extend the Transition Phase of the lease by an additional six months. This is not yet approved — Council will consider and decide on the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
ECC - LA AMENDMENT - 9048 STORMONT AVENUE
Purpose: To seek Council approval to amend the Lease Agreement (LA) with Pacific Assistance Dogs Society (PADS) to extend the Transition Phase for an additional six-month term at 9048 Stormont Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of Burnaby's 10-year parks and rec plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the final phase of the 'Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan.' This proposed 10-year plan would guide how the city builds and improves parks, community centres, and cultural spaces. Council will consider the plan at an upcoming meeting, along with feedback from recent public engagement sessions. If approved, the plan would set priorities for new facilities and upgrades across Burnaby over the next decade.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote date — if approved, this plan will shape future park and recreation projects that could affect your business location or operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - MAKING LIFE BETTER - PRC PLAN - FINAL PHASE (PHASE 4)
Purpose: To seek approval of the final Making Life Better: Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan, and to inform on the recent public engagement and revisions made to the Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider 2025 Canada Community-Building Fund update
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider an update on the 2025 Canada Community-Building Fund. This is a federal program that provides money to municipalities for infrastructure projects like roads, transit, and water systems. The update will outline how much funding Burnaby is expected to receive and how it can be spent. No final decision has been made yet — Council will review the information and may vote on how to allocate the funds at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
FMC - 2025 CANADA COMMUNITY-BUILDING FUND UPDATE
Purpose: To provide an update on the Federal Canada Community Building Fund Program for 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review major civic project updates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby as of May 2026. This is a proposed review of ongoing projects, not a final decision. The update will inform council about progress, timelines, and any changes to major city-building initiatives. Business owners should watch for potential impacts on construction, development, and local infrastructure that could affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Monitor council decisions following this update — project changes could affect your business location or supply chain.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MAY 2026
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026 for development
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw called the 'Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026'. This proposed bylaw would set new rules for how developers must provide or pay for essential services — like roads, water pipes, sewers, and streetlights — when they build on land in Burnaby. The goal is to make sure new developments pay their fair share for the public infrastructure they need. This is a first reading, meaning it is an early step in the approval process. The bylaw has not been passed yet, and council will need to vote on it again before it becomes law.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for future council meetings and public hearings on this bylaw — it could affect your development costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14829 - Burnaby Works and Services Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to respect provision of works and services in respect to development of land

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would create a new reserve fund called the 'Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund'. This fund would hold money paid by developers instead of building certain public works (like roads or sewers) themselves. The money would then be used by the city to pay for those works. This is a first reading of the bylaw, meaning it is still a proposal and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14830 - Burnaby Works and Services Payment-in-Lieu Reserve Fund Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish a Works and Services Payment-In-Lieu Reserve Fund

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby fee changes for works and services up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the fees the city charges for certain works and services. The proposed amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026) would update the fees listed under the 'works and services' section. This means businesses that need city permits, inspections, or other services related to construction, development, or municipal works could see their costs go up or down. The exact fee changes are not detailed in this agenda item, but the bylaw amendment is the first step toward approving them. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, these fee changes could affect your next permit or service application.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14831 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (works & services)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026 to regulate signs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the first reading of the Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026, a proposed bylaw that would regulate signs across the city. If approved, this bylaw could change what signs businesses can display, where they can be placed, and how they must be maintained. The decision is not final yet — Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any public hearing dates — this could affect your business signage.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14834 - Burnaby Sign Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to regulate signs in Burnaby

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment on sign enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, specifically regarding signs. This bylaw sets the rules for how the city can issue tickets and fines for bylaw violations. If approved, this change would update how sign-related infractions are enforced. The amendment is at its first reading stage, meaning it has been introduced but not yet passed. A final decision has not been made.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how sign violations are ticketed and fined for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14835 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (signs)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set out the rules and steps for how development applications (like building permits, rezoning, and subdivision requests) are reviewed and approved in Burnaby. If passed, it will replace the current procedures and change how developers and property owners submit and process their applications. The vote is scheduled for the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this bylaw will change how you submit development applications in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026 final adoption vote

agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14824 - Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to implement procedures for review and approval of development applications

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Building Bylaw amendment for development procedures
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016. This proposed bylaw would change the rules and procedures for how development applications are processed in the city. If approved, it could affect how builders, developers, and property owners submit plans and get permits. The exact changes to the development procedures are detailed in the proposed amendment.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could change how you submit development applications in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14825 - Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Building Bylaw 2016 (development procedures)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby development fees bylaw amendment up for final vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change the fees and charges related to development procedures — for example, building permits, rezoning applications, or subdivision fees. If approved, the new fee schedule would take effect after the vote. The amendment is listed as item 11.2.3 on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Check the council meeting date and watch for the decision — new development fees could affect your project costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby development fees bylaw amendment — final adoption vote at council meeting

#14826 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Agreement for 7880 & 7885 Newcombe Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 7880 and 7885 Newcombe Street. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the building's use as affordable rental housing for a set period. This proposed bylaw would secure those rental commitments for the new development.
agenda_briefs.source_body:

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Housing agreement for non-market rental at 6203 Marine & 6292 Byrnepark
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on final adoption of a Housing Agreement bylaw for a non-market rental housing development at 6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the rental rates and ensures the units stay affordable for a set period. This proposed bylaw would authorize the city to sign that agreement, making the project official.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the fees the City of Burnaby charges for development procedures. This includes things like building permits, rezoning applications, and other development-related services. The proposed bylaw is called the 'Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026.' If approved, it would update the current fee schedule. The exact new fees are not listed in this agenda item, but the decision will affect anyone applying for development approvals in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new fees will apply to your next development application.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby housing agreement for non-market rental at Marine/Byrnepark
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would authorize a Housing Agreement for a non-market rental housing development at 6203 Marine Drive and 6292 Byrnepark Drive. A Housing Agreement is a legal contract between the city and the developer that locks in the building's use as affordable rental housing for a set period. If approved, this would secure long-term below-market rents for tenants in the new building.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if adopted, this could affect rental availability and affordability in the Marine/Byrnepark area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
